heading: Empty cargo containers and barges
label: 55107 Empty cargo containers and barges
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)5510746 App.:883 (6th proviso).(6th proviso), ; , ; , , ; , , ; , , .June 5, 1920, ch. 250, § 2741 Stat. 999July 2, 1935, ch. 35549 Stat. 442Pub. L. 92–163, § 185 Stat. 486Sept. 21, 1965Aug. 11, 1968Nov. 23, 1971In subsection (a), before paragraph (1), the words “by vessels of the United States not qualified to engage in the coastwise trade, or by vessels of foreign registry” are omitted as unnecessary. In paragraph (4), the words “by the Secretary of the Treasury” are omitted as unnecessary because the section referred to provides who administers it.

L. 92–163, § 185 Stat. 486Sept. 21, 1965Aug. 11, 1968Nov. 23, 1971In subsection (a), before paragraph (1), the words “by vessels of the United States not qualified to engage in the coastwise trade, or by vessels of foreign registry” are omitted as unnecessary. In paragraph (4), the words “by the Secretary of the Treasury” are omitted as unnecessary because the section referred to provides who administers it.

children.text: empty cargo vans, empty lift vans, or empty shipping tanks;
equipment for use with cargo vans, lift vans, or shipping tanks;
empty barges specifically designed for carriage aboard a vessel and equipment (except propulsion equipment) for use with those barges;
empty instruments for international traffic exempted from the customs laws under section 322(a) of the Tariff Act of 1930 (); or19 U.S.C. 1322(a)

children.heading: .—Paragraphs (1)–(4)
children.label: 1 .—Paragraphs (1)–(4)
children.text: Paragraphs (1)–(4) of subsection (a) apply only if the items named are owned or leased by the owner or operator of the vessel and transported for its use in handling its cargo in foreign trade.

children.label: A
children.text: owned or leased by the owner or operator of the vessel or by the stevedoring company having the contract for the loading or unloading of the vessel; and

children.label: B
children.text: transported without charge for use in the handling of cargo in foreign trade.

children.heading: .—Reciprocity Requirement for Foreign Vessels
children.label: c .—Reciprocity Requirement for Foreign Vessels
children.text: This section applies to a vessel of foreign registry only if the Secretary of Homeland Security finds, based on information from the Secretary of State, that the government of the nation of registry extends reciprocal privileges to vessels of the United States.
